steps.title: Identify the absence signal
steps.content:
The passage states “they did not observe even one” — the blank needs a species and time period where the graph shows zero individuals.

Reading the graph: barred flagtail (solid line) shows 15, 10, 8, and 16 individuals across the four months; combtooth blenny (dotted line) shows 62, 3, 3, and 2 individuals.

Only the lined rockskipper (dashed line with squares) sits at zero for all four months — January, April, July, and October.

steps.title: Match the zero readings
steps.content:
The lined rockskipper is the only species that was entirely absent (zero observations) from the tide pool.

Choice D correctly names this species and specifically references two of the months—April and July 2001—where the graph shows zero individuals observed.

Choices A, B, and C all cite months where their respective species were actually present in measurable numbers, making them factually incorrect.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
